verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multi-pass transmembrane protein but resides in the autophagy machinery — trafficking between trans-Golgi network, recycling endosomes, and autophagosomal/pre-autophagosomal structures. It is the only known transmembrane core ATG protein but its steady-state localization is endomembrane (Golgi/endosomes), not plasma membrane. No documented surface display under stress, no shedding, no lineage-restricted PM pool, no lysosomal exocytosis route described, no surface-directed therapeutic program. Cell-state-induced: autophagy induction redistributes ATG9A among endomembranes, not to PM. Tissue-restricted: none. Lysosomal exocytosis: not reported. Dual-localization: no validated PM pool by surface biotinylation/flow. Stable-attachment: N/A (integral membrane, not secreted).
